status: runtime boundary audited; MCP/CLI planning, metadata-only availability probes, response normalization contracts, disabled client request scaffold, closed execution gate, health preflight plan, executable client harness plan, dispatch adapter boundary, opt-in configuration surfaces, unavailable error taxonomy, integration fixture harness, dispatch registration preflight, disabled executable adapter registration scaffold, disabled adapter registry manifest, final disabled enablement checklist, implementation slice audit, health/no-op contract fixtures, no-op service host scaffold, host lifecycle test fixtures, package manifest scaffold, package creation guardrails, package file templates, package workspace wiring, package build verification, package materialization checklist, package creation dry-run summary, package creation file manifest, package materialization approval gate, package materialization execution dry-run, package materialization write contract, package materialization rollback contract, package materialization verification manifest, package materialization final approval checklist, package materialization explicit approval token, package materialization approval audit trail, package materialization approval replay guard, package materialization approval expiry policy, package materialization approval revocation policy, package materialization approval scope binding policy, package materialization approval operator confirmation policy, and package materialization approval emergency stop policy registered; runtime execution blocked

decisionMatrix.reason: The MCP server is a Node TypeScript process without the Penpot ClojureScript render-wasm worker, browser canvas, rasterizer iframe, font, and asset runtime required to produce faithful thumbnail PNG bytes.
The browser worker already owns dashboard file thumbnail rendering, but it is tied to a live frontend session and is not a global background or CLI-safe execution boundary.
The exporter can drive Playwright screenshots for export previews, but it does not currently implement dashboard thumbnail data loading, cache policy, or backend thumbnail persistence semantics.
The backend owns permission checks, source data, storage, and persistence RPCs, but it does not render PNG bytes.
A dedicated renderer service can host the browser/WASM/rasterizer runtime, reuse backend thumbnail RPCs, persist cache entries, and return normalized resource metadata for MCP and CLI callers.

shape.resourceUri: backend persisted thumbnail resource URI when available
shape.downloadUri: absolute authenticated download URI when available

cli.localFileWrites: only when a future --output path is supplied
cli.shape: same resource metadata as MCP plus optional output metadata

frame.resourceSource: requires service or backend URI normalization

cacheRefresh.reuse: May return an existing resource only when the service can prove the file revn or tagged object key is current.
cacheRefresh.refresh: Must render a fresh PNG, persist it through the target-specific backend command, and return the persisted resource metadata.

auth.filePersist: create-file-thumbnail currently accepts read permission for dashboard thumbnail updates.
auth.service: The renderer service must use the caller session/token and must not mint unaudited storage writes.

integrationTestPlan: file refresh renders PNG, persists through create-file-thumbnail, and returns resource metadata
file reuse proves cache freshness before skipping render
tagged frame refresh loads frame source data and normalizes create-file-object-thumbnail media resource
MCP returns resource metadata without server-local file writes
CLI --output downloads only after normalized resource metadata exists
missing endpoint, missing opt-in, and missing integration tests fail before network dispatch

healthPreflight.expected: 200 application/json body with status, renderer, and version

resultHandling: normalize service success into resource metadata
keep MCP local file writes disabled
allow CLI output only after resource downloadUri exists

requiredBeforeDispatch: closed gate fixture passes without fetch
health unavailable and invalid fixtures prevent render POST
render success fixture normalizes MCP resource metadata
render success fixture gates CLI --output download on downloadUri
service error fixture maps stable taxonomy codes and retryability
auth fixture proves token values are not serialized

noOpBehavior: do not call exporter or plugin runtimes

noOpGuarantees: do not mutate command runtime adapter registry
do not register executable MCP handlers
do not register executable CLI handlers
do not call renderer-service network endpoints
do not write local files

requires: file thumbnail cache reuse and refresh fixtures
tagged frame source data and resource normalization
explicit opt-in and endpoint config validation
MCP metadata-only returns and CLI output download gating

testStrategy: Keep command-runtime descriptor tests asserting render.thumbnail only exposes the renderer-service planning adapter until execution exists.
Add renderer-service contract tests for file and tagged-frame inputs, cache policies, PNG metadata, and resource normalization.
MCP planning tests cover dry-run metadata, unavailable execution, adapter rejection, missing frame targets, and no runtime calls.
Metadata-only availability tests cover endpoint configuration, health endpoint derivation, probe timeout, and unavailable diagnostics without network calls.
Response normalization tests cover resource URI derivation, download URI resolution, renderer metadata, and retryable error payloads.
Disabled client request scaffold tests cover POST metadata, audit headers, auth-forwarding header names, request body mapping, and dispatch false.
Execution gate tests cover explicit opt-in, required config, integration-test blockers, failure modes, and dispatch false.
Health preflight and client harness tests cover GET metadata, closed-gate no-network behavior, sequence ordering, and dispatch false.
Dispatch adapter boundary tests cover config precedence, gate/preflight consumption, result/error mapping, and no-dispatch defaults.
Opt-in configuration tests cover CLI flag, MCP arg, environment fallback, invalid values, diagnostics, and no-dispatch defaults.
Integration fixture harness tests cover closed gate, health failure, render success, service error, MCP metadata, CLI output gating, and token-safe auth without dispatch.
Dispatch registration preflight tests cover final readiness checks and hard-disabled runtime registration.
Executable adapter registration scaffold tests cover consumed preflight/boundary/request metadata and no-op behavior.
Adapter registry manifest tests cover entrypoint wiring and metadata-only registry guarantees.
Enablement checklist tests cover final blocked runtime gates and no-dispatch readiness.
Implementation slice audit tests cover the selected health/no-op contract and keep dispatch, network, runtime registration, and local file writes disabled.
Health/no-op contract fixture tests cover health OK/unavailable and thumbnail.render no-op responses without dispatch, runtime registration, or local file writes.
No-op service host scaffold tests cover host config, planned routes, lifecycle, observability, and no process startup.
Host lifecycle test fixtures cover start, stop, readiness, supervision, logs, and errors without spawning a process or binding ports.
Package manifest scaffold tests cover future package metadata, scripts, exports, dependencies, workspace integration, and no file creation.
Package creation guardrails tests cover required creation checks and keep package/workspace mutations disabled.
Package file template tests cover planned package.json, tsconfig, source, and test shapes without materializing files.
Package workspace wiring tests cover planned workspace entries, root scripts, lockfile touchpoints, and no manifest mutation.
Add CLI smoke tests only after the service exists, covering dry-run, resource metadata, --output download, and missing-token errors.
Package build verification tests cover planned filtered build, type-check, test commands, expected artifacts, and no command execution.
Package materialization checklist tests cover planned file/workspace batches, readiness checklist, commit boundary, rollback plan, and no file materialization.
Package creation dry-run summary tests cover would-create, would-modify, would-generate, would-run, blocked-until, and no file writes.
